title: Sensor Replacement on Conveyor System
purpose: To provide step-by-step instructions for safely replacing a defective sensor on the conveyor system to ensure proper operation.
scope: This instruction covers the replacement of the sensor on the conveyor system, including safety precautions, sensor removal, installation, adjustment, and testing.

safety_precautions.precaution: Wear hard hat, eye goggles, and gloves
safety_precautions.reason: To protect against potential physical injury during maintenance

safety_precautions.precaution: Ensure lockout of power by confirming clicking sound and minimal machine movement when pulled
safety_precautions.reason: To prevent accidental machine startup and electrical hazards

procedure_steps.expected_result: Conveyor is fully stopped and no movement is detected.
procedure_steps.safety_note: Ensure the conveyor has come to a complete stop before proceeding.

procedure_steps.instruction: Lock out the power supply to the conveyor system and verify lockout by listening for the clicking sound and gently pulling the machine to confirm only a small give.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Power is securely locked out and machine cannot start unexpectedly.
procedure_steps.safety_note: This step is critical to prevent accidental startup and injury.

procedure_steps.instruction: Remove the sensor cover to access the defective sensor.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Sensor cover is safely removed without damage.

procedure_steps.instruction: Remove the defective sensor from its mounting.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Defective sensor is fully detached and ready for replacement.

procedure_steps.instruction: Install the new sensor (part number XYc1234d) in the correct position.
procedure_steps.expected_result: New sensor is properly mounted and aligned.

procedure_steps.instruction: Adjust the sensor position as needed to ensure proper operation.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Sensor is correctly positioned for accurate detection.

procedure_steps.instruction: Tighten all nuts and bolts securing the sensor to a torque of 1 Nm using a torque wrench.
procedure_steps.expected_result: All fasteners are tightened to the specified torque.

procedure_steps.instruction: Check the sensor signal on the Human Machine Interface (HMI) to verify correct operation.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Sensor signal is displayed correctly on the HMI.

procedure_steps.instruction: Run a test cycle of the conveyor system to confirm the sensor replacement was successful.
procedure_steps.expected_result: Conveyor operates normally with the new sensor functioning correctly.

quality_checks.acceptance_criteria: Sensor signal must be stable and indicate proper detection.

quality_checks.check: Torque of sensor mounting nuts and bolts
quality_checks.acceptance_criteria: All fasteners tightened to 1 Nm as specified.
Conveyor runs without faults and sensor triggers as expected.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Accidental startup of conveyor during maintenance
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Implement lockout/tagout procedures and verify lockout by physical checks.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Injury from sharp edges or moving parts
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Wear personal protective equipment including hard hat, eye goggles, and gloves.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Improper sensor installation causing conveyor malfunction
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Follow torque specifications and verify sensor signal on HMI before operation.

missing_information.why_it_matters: Proper sensor alignment is critical for accurate operation and is not described.

missing_information.item: Specific tools besides torque wrench (e.g., screwdrivers, wrenches)
missing_information.why_it_matters: Additional tools required for sensor cover removal and sensor replacement are not listed.
Details on how to run and evaluate the test cycle are missing, which affects validation.

assumptions: The maintenance technician has access to and knowledge of lockout/tagout procedures.
The sensor cover removal does not require special tools beyond common hand tools.
The conveyor system's HMI provides real-time sensor status signals.
